channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Blanche Faces Crucial Hurdle After Rocky Hearing
item.description: Even a single Republican “no” vote would block Mr. Blanche’s nomination from consideration by the full Senate, which could sink his confirmation.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush, Devlin Barrett, Michael Gold and Alan Feuer
item.media:description: Todd Blanche on Wednesday at his confirmation hearing to be attorney general. It is not clear when the Senate Judiciary Committee will schedule a vote.

item.title: What to Know About Todd Blanche’s Order Granting Trump Immunity From I.R.S. Audits
item.description: Acting attorney general Todd Blanche’s decision to grant President Trump protection from I.R.S. audits was a central issue in his confirmation hearing on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Duehren
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump’s extraordinary protection from the I.R.S. is expected to be a central issue in the confirmation hearing on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump’s Latest C.D.C. Nominee Vows Not to ‘Betray the Science’
item.description: Dr. Erica Schwartz told senators in a confirmation hearing that she did not think Robert F. Kennedy Jr. or President Trump would ask her to do anything to hurt public health. Some senators were incredulous.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
item.media:description: Dr. Erica Schwartz, a former deputy surgeon general, is President Trump’s third nominee in two years to lead the C.D.C.

item.title: War Risk for Businesses Will Mean Higher Prices No Matter What Happens
item.description: Doing business in a more dangerous and unpredictable world will cost more, pushing up the price of everything from food to electronics.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Cohen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Linh Pham for The New York Times
item.media:description: Supply chain disruptions have raised costs and disrupted manufacturing across Southeast Asia.

item.title: U.S. Strikes Hit Greater Tunb Island in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. military said on Wednesday it had attacked Iranian cruise missile storage and launch sites on Greater Tunb, one of several small islands in the Strait of Hormuz subject to a decades-old territorial dispute.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Vessels at anchor in May off the coast of Oman, about 60 miles from Greater Tunb island in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: F.B.I. Searches Houston ICE Shooting Van for Drugs
item.description: Federal investigators believe illegal drugs were inside the vehicle, though the immigration agents involved in the encounter do not appear to have known that.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman
item.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
item.media:description: Family members embraced next to a makeshift memorial for Lorenzo Salgado Araujo at the location where he was shot by an I.C.E. agent in Houston.

item.title: Trump Pressures ICE to Resume Traffic Stops After They Were Halted Over Fatal Shootings
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement had ordered its officers to halt most vehicle stops across the country after they shot two people over the past week.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.media:description: In a post on social media on Wednesday, President Trump defended the traffic stops and urged ICE to “go back and do your very important job.”

item.title: Court Filings Describe Ongoing Contact Between ICE and Iran on Deportations
item.description: In a sworn declaration, a lawyer says an Iranian official reported receiving regular dossiers on Iranian detainees from Immigration and Customs Enforcement for months.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague
item.media:credit: Kent Nishimura for The New York Times
item.media:description: According to one filing, the Iranian official indicated the Trump administration’s goal was to “get rid of all Iranians” held in immigration detention in the United States.

item.title: Ukraine Pounds Russian Ships in Its Campaign to Cut Off Crimea
item.description: After striking roads and railways, Kyiv is now focusing on sea routes as it tries to disrupt fuel supplies and pressure Russia to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Nataliya Vasilyeva, Marc Santora and Cassandra Vinograd
item.media:credit: Commander of Unmanned Aerial Systems Force, via Reuters
item.media:description: A screenshot taken from footage provided by Ukraine’s Unmanned Systems Forces showed a drone flying toward a ship at port in the city of Kerch in Crimea in early July.

item.title: Senators Grill Justice Dept. Officials on New York Times Subpoenas
item.description: Democrats questioned the acting attorney general, Todd Blanche, and a U.S. attorney about subpoenas issued to Times journalists who reported on security concerns with the president’s new plane.
item.dc:creator: Michael M. Grynbaum
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times, Alex Kent/The New York Times

item.title: Our Top Editor on Subpoenas of Times Journalists
item.description: The Trump administration issued subpoenas to reporters who wrote about security concerns involving the new Air Force One. Joe Kahn, the executive editor of The New York Times, responds to what he calls a “naked attempt to intimidate” the newspaper.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Kahn, Jillian Eugenios, Megan DiTrolio, Santiago García Muñoz, Alejandro Soto Goico, Zach Caldwell, Luke Piotrowski and Mike Abrams
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: House G.O.P. Releases Budget to Unlock $95 Billion for Iran War and SAVE Act
item.description: The plan faces a rocky path amid Republican divisions and would pave the way for votes months before the midterm elections to fund a war that polls show is deeply unpopular.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Representative Jodey Arrington, Republican of Texas and the chairman of the House Budget Committee. His panel was set to vote on the plan on Thursday.

item.title: Treasury to Begin Minting $1 Gold Trump Coin Featuring President’s Face
item.description: Critics question whether the coins are legal and argue that President Trump is using the currency to celebrate himself.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport

item.title: ‘The Odyssey’ Review: Passion in Every Frame
item.description: Few directors close the divide between the art film and the blockbuster like Christopher Nolan.
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis

item.title: Archaeologists Unearth a 2,400-Year-Old Sanctuary for the Worship of Odysseus
item.description: The ruins of a 2,400-year-old complex suggest that, to the Greeks of antiquity, Homer’s protagonist was not just a character in a poem but a figure of genuine devotion.
item.dc:creator: Franz Lidz
item.media:credit: Fine Art Images/Heritage Images, via Getty Images
item.media:description: A 19th-century painting by the Swiss painter Arnold Böcklin depicting Odysseus fleeing the Cyclops Polyphemus.

item.title: He Wanted to Be John Travolta. Instead, He Became ‘Kaves.’
item.description: As a child, Michael McLeer wanted to be like John Travolta in “Saturday Night Fever” and escape Brooklyn. A tragedy in the neighborhood changed his mind.
item.dc:creator: Abby Ellin and Lanna Apisukh

item.title: Flash Flood Emergency Is Issued in Texas After Days of Pounding Rain
item.description: Roads became rivers on Wednesday as emergency officials in towns around San Antonio raced to rescue those who were stranded.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones and Christine Hauser

item.title: Wildfire Smoke Spreads Across the Northeast as Temperatures Spike
item.description: A heat dome is responsible for miserably high temperatures, and it’s also helping to push smoke from wildfires to the east.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones and Mark Walker

item.title: Canada Wildfire Air Pollution and Heat Wave Combo Pose Heightened Health Risks
item.description: Days that are both extremely hot and polluted come with higher risks of respiratory ailments and other health risks.
item.dc:creator: Claire Brown and Christina Kelso
item.media:credit: Laura Proctor/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A hazy street in Toronto as wildfire smoke blanketed the city on Wednesday.

item.title: Inside Maine Democrats’ Search for a New Senate Nominee After Graham Platner’s Exit
item.description: Democratic candidates are hastily building bare-bones campaigns and forgoing traditional outreach like TV ads in a sprint to appeal to party delegates, rather than Maine voters.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Several Maine Democrats are jockeying to become the party’s new Senate nominee after Graham Platner, who won the midterm primary, dropped out last week.

item.title: Elon Musk Funds Vivek Ramaswamy’s Bid for Ohio Governor Despite Their DOGE Clashes
item.description: Elon Musk gave $5 million to back Vivek Ramaswamy’s campaign for Ohio governor, seemingly moving past tensions around the effort to downsize the federal work force.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times; Eric Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy developed a frosty relationship after President Trump named them as the leaders of the so-called Department of Government Efficiency.

item.title: Trump Endorses Mike Lindell, MyPillow Founder, for Minnesota Governor
item.description: He is a close ally of President Trump and has been a leading proponent of the election denial movement, which falsely claims that voting machines are often rigged.
item.dc:creator: Ernesto Londoño
item.media:description: Mike Lindell at the Conservative Political Action Conference in Grapevine, Texas, in March.

item.title: New Jersey Couple Aids Meteorite Discovery After it Crashes Through Their Roof
item.description: A New Jersey couple helped scientists uncover the makeup of an unusual asteroid after it fell into their bedroom.
item.dc:creator: Robin George Andrews

item.title: How Worried Should You Be About Cyclospora?
item.description: Don’t stop eating fruits and vegetables.
item.dc:creator: Emily Oster
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

item.title: Are We Still Supposed to Take This Conservative Legal Theory Seriously?
item.description: Has the court learned nothing?
item.dc:creator: Linda Greenhouse

item.title: Small Tribes Say They Lack the Clout to Secure Federal Recognition
item.description: The federal government can confer health care, education, housing and casino rights, but for small bands like many in the Pacific Northwest, recognition takes political muscle they don’t have.
item.dc:creator: Anna Griffin
item.media:description: “We refuse to say we don’t have federal recognition; the federal government cannot tell me we are or are not Chinook.” Members of the Chinook Indian Nation near Bay Center, Wash.
